mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with t-stat > 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer price inflation and the federal funds rate indicate aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces no statistically significant negative alpha compared to single-series signals over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index during post-shock recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery regimes, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real personal income growth directly boosts corporate revenue expectations, creating a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year housing starts decline exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and household wealth, triggering negative equity market returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 5% year-over-year
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods following a >5% YoY drop in housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no predictive power for 20-day returns after controlling for market momentum, with ICIR below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation environments, rapid CPI increases erode real corporate margins and force anticipatory monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes fail to predict negative 5-day returns during high inflation periods, with mean forward return exceeding +0.1%.
